NKJV.1: Now in those days, when the number of the disciples was multiplying, there arose a complaint against the Hebrews by the Hellenists, because their widows were neglected in the daily distribution.
NKJV.2: Then the twelve summoned the multitude of the disciples and said, “It is not desirable that we should leave the word of God and serve tables.
NKJV.3: Therefore, brethren, seek out from among you seven men of good reputation, full of the Holy Spirit and wisdom, whom we may appoint over this business;
NKJV.4: but we will give ourselves continually to prayer and to the ministry of the word.”
NKJV.5: And the saying pleased the whole multitude. And they chose Stephen, a man full of faith and the Holy Spirit, and Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolas, a proselyte from Antioch,
NKJV.6: whom they set before the apostles; and when they had prayed, they laid hands on them.
NKJV.7: Then the word of God spread, and the number of the disciples multiplied greatly in Jerusalem, and a great many of the priests were obedient to the faith.
NKJV.8: And Stephen, full of faith and power, did great wonders and signs among the people.
NKJV.9: Then there arose some from what is called the Synagogue of the Freedmen (Cyrenians, Alexandrians, and those from Cilicia and Asia), disputing with Stephen.
NKJV.10: And they were not able to resist the wisdom and the Spirit by which he spoke.
NKJV.11: Then they secretly induced men to say, “We have heard him speak blasphemous words against Moses and God.”
NKJV.12: And they stirred up the people, the elders, and the scribes; and they came upon him, seized him, and brought him to the council.
NKJV.13: They also set up false witnesses who said, “This man does not cease to speak blasphemous words against this holy place and the law;
NKJV.14: for we have heard him say that this Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs which Moses delivered to us.”
NKJV.15: And all who sat in the council, looking steadfastly at him, saw his face as the face of an angel.

ESV.1: Now in these days when the disciples were increasing in number, a complaint by the Hellenists  arose against the Hebrews because their widows were being neglected in the daily distribution.
ESV.2: And the twelve summoned the full number of the disciples and said, “It is not right that we should give up preaching the word of God to serve tables.
ESV.3: Therefore, brothers,  pick out from among you seven men of good repute, full of the Spirit and of wisdom, whom we will appoint to this duty.
ESV.4: But we will devote ourselves to prayer and to the ministry of the word.”
ESV.5: And what they said pleased the whole gathering, and they chose Stephen, a man full of faith and of the Holy Spirit, and Philip, and Prochorus, and Nicanor, and Timon, and Parmenas, and Nicolaus, a proselyte of Antioch.
ESV.6: These they set before the apostles, and they prayed and laid their hands on them.
ESV.7: And the word of God continued to increase, and the number of the disciples multiplied greatly in Jerusalem, and a great many of the priests became obedient to the faith.
ESV.8: And Stephen, full of grace and power, was doing great wonders and signs among the people.
ESV.9: Then some of those who belonged to the synagogue of the Freedmen (as it was called), and of the Cyrenians, and of the Alexandrians, and of those from Cilicia and Asia, rose up and disputed with Stephen.
ESV.10: But they could not withstand the wisdom and the Spirit with which he was speaking.
ESV.11: Then they secretly instigated men who said, “We have heard him speak blasphemous words against Moses and God.”
ESV.12: And they stirred up the people and the elders and the scribes, and they came upon him and seized him and brought him before the council,
ESV.13: and they set up false witnesses who said, “This man never ceases to speak words against this holy place and the law,
ESV.14: for we have heard him say that this Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place and will change the customs that Moses delivered to us.”
ESV.15: And gazing at him, all who sat in the council saw that his face was like the face of an angel.

NASB.1: Now at this time while the disciples were increasing [in number], a complaint arose on the part of the Hellenistic [Jews] against the [native] Hebrews, because their widows were being overlooked in the daily serving [of food].
NASB.2: So the twelve summoned the congregation of the disciples and said, 'It is not desirable for us to neglect the word of God in order to serve tables.
NASB.3: 'Therefore, brethren, select from among you seven men of good reputation, full of the Spirit and of wisdom, whom we may put in charge of this task.
NASB.4: 'But we will devote ourselves to prayer and to the ministry of the word.'
NASB.5: The statement found approval with the whole congregation; and they chose Stephen, a man full of faith and of the Holy Spirit, and Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas and Nicolas, a proselyte from Antioch.
NASB.6: And these they brought before the apostles; and after praying, they laid their hands on them.
NASB.7: The word of God kept on spreading; and the number of the disciples continued to increase greatly in Jerusalem, and a great many of the priests were becoming obedient to the faith.
NASB.8: And Stephen, full of grace and power, was performing great wonders and signs among the people.
NASB.9: But some men from what was called the Synagogue of the Freedmen, [including] both Cyrenians and Alexandrians, and some from Cilicia and Asia, rose up and argued with Stephen.
NASB.10: But they were unable to cope with the wisdom and the Spirit with which he was speaking.
NASB.11: Then they secretly induced men to say, 'We have heard him speak blasphemous words against Moses and [against] God.'
NASB.12: And they stirred up the people, the elders and the scribes, and they came up to him and dragged him away and brought him before the Council.
NASB.13: They put forward false witnesses who said, 'This man incessantly speaks against this holy place and the Law;
NASB.14: for we have heard him say that this Nazarene, Jesus, will destroy this place and alter the customs which Moses handed down to us.'
NASB.15: And fixing their gaze on him, all who were sitting in the Council saw his face like the face of an angel.

NLT.1: But as the believers rapidly multiplied, there were rumblings of discontent. The Greek-speaking believers complained about the Hebrew-speaking believers, saying that their widows were being discriminated against in the daily distribution of food.
NLT.2: So the Twelve called a meeting of all the believers. They said, “We apostles should spend our time teaching the word of God, not running a food program.
NLT.3: And so, brothers, select seven men who are well respected and are full of the Spirit and wisdom. We will give them this responsibility.
NLT.4: Then we apostles can spend our time in prayer and teaching the word.”
NLT.5: Everyone liked this idea, and they chose the following: Stephen (a man full of faith and the Holy Spirit), Philip, Procorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolas of Antioch (an earlier convert to the Jewish faith).
NLT.6: These seven were presented to the apostles, who prayed for them as they laid their hands on them.
NLT.7: So God’s message continued to spread. The number of believers greatly increased in Jerusalem, and many of the Jewish priests were converted, too.
NLT.8: Stephen, a man full of God’s grace and power, performed amazing miracles and signs among the people.
NLT.9: But one day some men from the Synagogue of Freed Slaves, as it was called, started to debate with him. They were Jews from Cyrene, Alexandria, Cilicia, and the province of Asia.
NLT.10: None of them could stand against the wisdom and the Spirit with which Stephen spoke.
NLT.11: So they persuaded some men to lie about Stephen, saying, “We heard him blaspheme Moses, and even God.”
NLT.12: This roused the people, the elders, and the teachers of religious law. So they arrested Stephen and brought him before the high council.
NLT.13: The lying witnesses said, “This man is always speaking against the holy Temple and against the law of Moses.
NLT.14: We have heard him say that this Jesus of Nazareth will destroy the Temple and change the customs Moses handed down to us.”
NLT.15: At this point everyone in the high council stared at Stephen, because his face became as bright as an angel’s.

WEB.1: Now in those days, when the number of the disciples was multiplying, a complaint arose from the Hellenists  against the Hebrews, because their widows were neglected in the daily service.
WEB.2: The twelve summoned the multitude of the disciples and said, “It is not appropriate for us to forsake the word of God and serve tables.
WEB.3: Therefore select from among you, brothers, seven men of good report, full of the Holy Spirit and of wisdom, whom we may appoint over this business.
WEB.4: But we will continue steadfastly in prayer and in the ministry of the word.”
WEB.5: These words pleased the whole multitude. They chose Stephen, a man full of faith and of the Holy Spirit, Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolaus, a proselyte of Antioch;
WEB.6: whom they set before the apostles. When they had prayed, they laid their hands on them.
WEB.7: The word of God increased and the number of the disciples multiplied in Jerusalem exceedingly. A great company of the priests were obedient to the faith.
WEB.8: Stephen, full of faith and power, performed great wonders and signs among the people.
WEB.9: But some of those who were of the synagogue called “The Libertines”, and of the Cyrenians, of the Alexandrians, and of those of Cilicia and Asia arose, disputing with Stephen.
WEB.10: They weren’t able to withstand the wisdom and the Spirit by which he spoke.
WEB.11: Then they secretly induced men to say, “We have heard him speak blasphemous words against Moses and God.”
WEB.12: They stirred up the people, the elders, and the scribes, and came against him and seized him, and brought him in to the council,
WEB.13: and set up false witnesses who said, “This man never stops speaking blasphemous words against this holy place and the law.
WEB.14: For we have heard him say that this Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place, and will change the customs which Moses delivered to us.”
WEB.15: All who sat in the council, fastening their eyes on him, saw his face like it was the face of an angel.

CSB17.1: In those days, as the disciples <sup>ⓑ</sup> were increasing in number, there arose a complaint by the Hellenistic Jews against the Hebraic Jews that their widows were being overlooked in the daily distribution. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.2: The Twelve summoned the whole company of the disciples and said, “It would not be right for us to give up preaching the word of God to wait on tables.
CSB17.3: Brothers and sisters, select from among you seven men of good reputation, <sup>ⓓ</sup> full of the Spirit <sup>ⓔ</sup> and wisdom, whom we can appoint to this duty.
CSB17.4: But we will devote ourselves to prayer and to the ministry of the word.”
CSB17.5: This proposal pleased the whole company. So they chose Stephen, <sup>ⓕ</sup> a man full of faith and the Holy Spirit, and Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolaus, a convert from Antioch. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.6: They had them stand before the apostles, who prayed <sup>ⓗ</sup> and laid their hands on <sup>ⓘ</sup> them.
CSB17.7: So the word of God spread, the disciples in Jerusalem increased <sup>ⓙ</sup> greatly in number, and a large group of priests became obedient to the faith.
CSB17.8: Now Stephen, full of grace and power, was performing great wonders and signs among the people.
CSB17.9: Opposition arose, however, from some members of the Freedmen’s Synagogue, composed of both Cyrenians and Alexandrians, and some from Cilicia and Asia, and they began to argue with Stephen.
CSB17.10: But they were unable to stand up against his wisdom and the Spirit by whom he was speaking.
CSB17.11: Then they secretly persuaded some men to say, “We heard him speaking blasphemous words against Moses and God.” <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.12: They stirred up the people, the elders, and the scribes; so they came, seized him, and took him to the Sanhedrin.
CSB17.13: They also presented false witnesses who said, “This man never stops speaking against this holy place and the law. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.14: For we heard him say that this Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs that Moses handed down to us.” <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.15: And all who were sitting in the Sanhedrin looked intently at him and saw that his face was like the face of an angel.

AMP.1: Now about this time, when the number of disciples was increasing, a complaint was made by the Hellenists (Greek-speaking Jews) against the [ native] Hebrews, because their widows were being overlooked in the daily serving of food.
AMP.2: So the Twelve called the disciples together and said, "It is not appropriate for us to neglect [teaching] the word of God in order to serve tables and manage the distribution of food.
AMP.3: Therefore, brothers, choose from among you seven men with good reputations [men of godly character and moral integrity], full of the Spirit and of wisdom, whom we may put in charge of this task.
AMP.4: But we will [continue to] devote ourselves [steadfastly] to prayer and to the ministry of the word."
AMP.5: The suggestion pleased the whole congregation; and they selected Stephen, a man full of faith [in Christ Jesus], and [filled with and led by] the Holy Spirit, and Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolas (Nikolaos), a proselyte (Gentile convert) from Antioch.
AMP.6: They brought these men before the apostles; and after praying, they laid their hands on them [to dedicate and commission them for this service].
AMP.7: And the message of God kept on growing and spreading, and the number of disciples continued to increase greatly in Jerusalem; and a large number of the priests were becoming obedient to the faith [accepting Jesus as Messiah and acknowledging Him as the Source of eternal salvation].
AMP.8: Now Stephen, full of grace (divine blessing, favor) and power, was doing great wonders and signs (attesting miracles) among the people.
AMP.9: However, some men from what was called the Synagogue of the Freedmen (freed Jewish slaves), both Cyrenians and Alexandrians, and some from Cilicia and [the province of] Asia, rose up and questioned and argued with Stephen.
AMP.10: But they were not able to successfully withstand and cope with the wisdom and the intelligence [and the power and inspiration] of the Spirit by whom he was speaking.
AMP.11: Then [to attack him another way] they secretly instructed men to say, "We have heard this man [Stephen] speak blasphemous (slanderous, sacrilegious, abusive) words against Moses and against God."
AMP.12: And they provoked and incited the people, as well as the elders and the scribes, and they came up to Stephen and seized him and brought him before the Council (Sanhedrin, Jewish High Court).
AMP.13: They presented false witnesses who said, "This man never stops speaking against this holy place and the Law [of Moses];
AMP.14: for we have heard him say that this Jesus the Nazarene will tear down this place and will change the traditions and customs which Moses handed down to us."
AMP.15: Then all those who were sitting in the Council, stared [intently] at him, and they saw that Stephen's face was like the face of an angel.

MSG.1: During this time, as the disciples were increasing in numbers by leaps and bounds, hard feelings developed among the Greek-speaking believers—"Hellenists"—toward the Hebrew-speaking believers because their widows were being discriminated against in the daily food lines.
MSG.2: So the Twelve called a meeting of the disciples. They said, "It wouldn't be right for us to abandon our responsibilities for preaching and teaching the Word of God to help with the care of the poor.
MSG.3: So, friends, choose seven men from among you whom everyone trusts, men full of the Holy Spirit and good sense, and we'll assign them this task.
MSG.4: Meanwhile, we'll stick to our assigned tasks of prayer and speaking God's Word."
MSG.5: The congregation thought this was a great idea. They went ahead and chose— Stephen, a man full of faith and the Holy Spirit, Philip, Procorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, Nicolas, a convert from Antioch.
MSG.6: Then they presented them to the apostles. Praying, the apostles laid on hands and commissioned them for their task.
MSG.7: The Word of God prospered. The number of disciples in Jerusalem increased dramatically. Not least, a great many priests submitted themselves to the faith.
MSG.8: Stephen, brimming with God's grace and energy, was doing wonderful things among the people, unmistakable signs that God was among them.
MSG.9: But then some men from the meeting place whose membership was made up of freed slaves, Cyrenians, Alexandrians, and some others from Cilicia and Asia, went up against him trying to argue him down.
MSG.10: But they were no match for his wisdom and spirit when he spoke.
MSG.11: So in secret they bribed men to lie: "We heard him cursing Moses and God."
MSG.12: That stirred up the people, the religious leaders, and religion scholars. They grabbed Stephen and took him before the High Council
MSG.13: They put forward their bribed witnesses to testify: "This man talks nonstop against this Holy Place and God's Law.
MSG.14: We even heard him say that Jesus of Nazareth would tear this place down and throw out all the customs Moses gave us."
MSG.15: As all those who sat on the High Council looked at Stephen, they found they couldn't take their eyes off him—his face was like the face of an angel!

NIV.1:
The Choosing of the Seven
In those days when the number of disciples was increasing, the Grecian Jews among them complained against the Hebraic Jews because their widows were being overlooked in the daily distribution of food.
NIV.2: So the Twelve gathered all the disciples together and said, “It would not be right for us to neglect the ministry of the word of God in order to wait on tables.
NIV.3: Brothers, choose seven men from among you who are known to be full of the Spirit and wisdom. We will turn this responsibility over to them
NIV.4: and will give our attention to prayer and the ministry of the word.”
NIV.5: This proposal pleased the whole group. They chose Stephen, a man full of faith and of the Holy Spirit; also Philip, Procorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolas from Antioch, a convert to Judaism.
NIV.6: They presented these men to the apostles, who prayed and laid their hands on them.
NIV.7: So the word of God spread. The number of disciples in Jerusalem increased rapidly, and a large number of priests became obedient to the faith.
NIV.8:
Stephen Seized
Now Stephen, a man full of God’s grace and power, did great wonders and miraculous signs among the people.
NIV.9: Opposition arose, however, from members of the Synagogue of the Freedmen (as it was called)—Jews of Cyrene and Alexandria as well as the provinces of Cilicia and Asia. These men began to argue with Stephen,
NIV.10: but they could not stand up against his wisdom or the Spirit by whom he spoke.
NIV.11: Then they secretly persuaded some men to say, “We have heard Stephen speak words of blasphemy against Moses and against God.”
NIV.12: So they stirred up the people and the elders and the teachers of the law. They seized Stephen and brought him before the Sanhedrin.
NIV.13: They produced false witnesses, who testified, “This fellow never stops speaking against this holy place and against the law.
NIV.14: For we have heard him say that this Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs Moses handed down to us.”
NIV.15: All who were sitting in the Sanhedrin looked intently at Stephen, and they saw that his face was like the face of an angel.

NRSVCE.1: Now during those days, when the disciples were increasing in number, the Hellenists complained against the Hebrews because their widows were being neglected in the daily distribution of food.
NRSVCE.2: And the twelve called together the whole community of the disciples and said, “It is not right that we should neglect the word of God in order to wait on tables.
NRSVCE.3: Therefore, friends, select from among yourselves seven men of good standing, full of the Spirit and of wisdom, whom we may appoint to this task,
NRSVCE.4: while we, for our part, will devote ourselves to prayer and to serving the word.”
NRSVCE.5: What they said pleased the whole community, and they chose Stephen, a man full of faith and the Holy Spirit, together with Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolaus, a proselyte of Antioch.
NRSVCE.6: They had these men stand before the apostles, who prayed and laid their hands on them.
NRSVCE.7: The word of God continued to spread; the number of the disciples increased greatly in Jerusalem, and a great many of the priests became obedient to the faith.
NRSVCE.8: Stephen, full of grace and power, did great wonders and signs among the people.
NRSVCE.9: Then some of those who belonged to the synagogue of the Freedmen (as it was called), Cyrenians, Alexandrians, and others of those from Cilicia and Asia, stood up and argued with Stephen.
NRSVCE.10: But they could not withstand the wisdom and the Spirit with which he spoke.
NRSVCE.11: Then they secretly instigated some men to say, “We have heard him speak blasphemous words against Moses and God.”
NRSVCE.12: They stirred up the people as well as the elders and the scribes; then they suddenly confronted him, seized him, and brought him before the council.
NRSVCE.13: They set up false witnesses who said, “This man never stops saying things against this holy place and the law;
NRSVCE.14: for we have heard him say that this Jesus of Nazareth will destroy this place and will change the customs that Moses handed on to us.”
NRSVCE.15: And all who sat in the council looked intently at him, and they saw that his face was like the face of an angel.

NET.1: Now in those days, when the disciples were growing in number, a complaint arose on the part of the Greek-speaking Jews against the native Hebraic Jews, because their widows were being overlooked in the daily distribution of food.
NET.2: So the twelve called the whole group of the disciples together and said, “It is not right for us to neglect the word of God to wait on tables.
NET.3: But carefully select from among you, brothers, seven men who are well-attested, full of the Spirit and of wisdom, whom we may put in charge of this necessary task.
NET.4: But we will devote ourselves to prayer and to the ministry of the word.”
NET.5: The proposal pleased the entire group, so they chose Stephen, a man full of faith and of the Holy Spirit, with Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and Nicolas, a Gentile convert to Judaism from Antioch.
NET.6: They stood these men before the apostles, who prayed and placed their hands on them.
NET.7: The word of God continued to spread, the number of disciples in Jerusalem increased greatly, and a large group of priests became obedient to the faith.
NET.8: Now Stephen, full of grace and power, was performing great wonders and miraculous signs among the people.
NET.9: But some men from the Synagogue of the Freedmen (as it was called), both Cyrenians and Alexandrians, as well as some from Cilicia and the province of Asia, stood up and argued with Stephen.
NET.10: Yet they were not able to resist the wisdom and the Spirit with which he spoke.
NET.11: Then they secretly instigated some men to say, “We have heard this man speaking blasphemous words against Moses and God.”
NET.12: They incited the people, the elders, and the experts in the law; then they approached Stephen, seized him, and brought him before the council.
NET.13: They brought forward false witnesses who said, “This man does not stop saying things against this holy place and the law.
NET.14: For we have heard him saying that Jesus the Nazarene will destroy this place and change the customs that Moses handed down to us.”
NET.15: All who were sitting in the council looked intently at Stephen and saw his face was like the face of an angel.
